topic_label: Diffusion Models × Natural Language × Genomic Sequences
thesis: Diffusion models began as a continuous-noise generative process for images but have rapidly extended to discrete tokens (text, DNA, RNA, protein sequences) and to high-dimensional structured biology. We argue that progress at this intersection will be driven by three coordinated developments: (i) continuous-time diffusion foundations (score-based modelling, classifier-free guidance, latent-space scaling) that supply the modelling vocabulary; (ii) discrete & language diffusion that adapts the framework to text and other token sequences and now reaches LLM scale; and (iii) biological sequence and structure diffusion that translates these advances into protein, DNA, and RNA design — closing the loop between natural-language conditioning and functional biomolecule generation.

paradigms.label: Continuous diffusion foundations
paradigms.summary: The modelling vocabulary: score-based generative modelling, discrete-time denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPM), and the continuous-time SDE framework; classifier-free guidance and latent-space scaling that made diffusion practical at modern scale.

claims.claim: Score-based generative modelling and denoising diffusion probabilistic models (NCSN, DDPM) establish a learnable noise-prediction objective whose reverse process samples high-quality data from a tractable Gaussian prior, giving the field its core modelling vocabulary.
claims.search_terms: denoising diffusion probabilistic models score-based generative noise prediction NCSN DDPM

claims.claim: Continuous-time score-based stochastic differential equations unify discrete-time DDPM with score-matching and make implicit, deterministic, and accelerated samplers (DDIM, probability-flow ODE) natural special cases.
claims.search_terms: score-based stochastic differential equation continuous time SDE generative modelling DDIM probability flow

claims.claim: Latent-space diffusion (LDM / Stable Diffusion) and classifier-free guidance are the two engineering innovations that made diffusion practical at modern scale — by moving the diffusion process into a perceptual / semantic latent and by enabling strong conditional generation without an explicit classifier.
claims.search_terms: latent diffusion classifier-free guidance Stable Diffusion conditional generation perceptual compression

paradigms.label: Discrete & language diffusion
paradigms.summary: Adapting diffusion to discrete tokens — first via D3PM-style discrete state Markov chains, then via score-entropy / masked diffusion that scales much better, and finally via LLM-scale discrete diffusion models that challenge autoregressive language modelling head-on.

claims.claim: Discrete-state diffusion (D3PM, DiffusionLM) defines a forward Markov chain over discrete tokens — uniform / absorbing / Gaussian transition kernels — and trains a reverse denoising model that can generate fluent text without an autoregressive factorisation.
claims.search_terms: discrete diffusion D3PM DiffusionLM text generation Markov chain absorbing state

claims.claim: Score-entropy discrete diffusion (SEDD) and masked diffusion language models (MDLM) replace the divergence-based discrete-diffusion losses with score-entropy / masked-token objectives that scale more reliably and close the perplexity gap with autoregressive baselines.
claims.search_terms: score entropy discrete diffusion SEDD masked diffusion language model MDLM ratio matching

claims.claim: LLM-scale discrete diffusion models (LLaDA, DiffuLLaMA, BeyondAR) demonstrate that non-autoregressive diffusion can match — and in some regimes exceed — autoregressive LLM performance at billion-parameter scale, while enabling parallel decoding and bidirectional context.
claims.search_terms: LLaDA DiffuLLaMA discrete diffusion large language model billion parameter non-autoregressive

paradigms.label: Biological sequence & structure diffusion
paradigms.summary: Translating diffusion into protein, DNA, and RNA design. Three sub-areas have reached production-grade utility: structure-conditioned protein backbone generation (RFdiffusion), protein-sequence diffusion language models (EvoDiff / DPLM), and nucleotide-sequence diffusion for DNA / RNA design (DNADiffusion, DiscDiff, RNAdiffusion, DRAKES).

claims.claim: Diffusion over 3D protein backbones (RFdiffusion-style) enables de novo design of binders, scaffolds, and symmetric assemblies that experimentally fold and function — bringing diffusion-as-generative-prior into the wet-lab design loop.
claims.search_terms: RFdiffusion protein backbone de novo design diffusion structure generation binder

claims.claim: Protein-sequence diffusion models (EvoDiff, DPLM) train discrete diffusion on amino-acid sequences and reach parity with — or exceed — protein language models on inverse-folding, generation, and design tasks while supporting flexible conditioning.
claims.search_terms: EvoDiff DPLM protein sequence diffusion amino acid generation inverse folding

claims.claim: Discrete diffusion for nucleotide sequences (DNADiffusion, DiscDiff, D3, RNAdiffusion, DRAKES) generates regulatory DNA, RNA aptamers, and codon-optimised CDSs with controllable function, demonstrating that the diffusion framework transfers cleanly from text to nucleic acids.
claims.search_terms: DNA diffusion RNA diffusion DiscDiff DNADiffusion regulatory sequence aptamer DRAKES

anchors.5: strong, direct, explicit support for THIS specific claim

venue_allowlist: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics

viewer.title: Diffusion Models × Natural Language × Genomic Sequences — Knowledge Graph
viewer.subtitle: Three coordinated developments at the intersection of diffusion-based generative modelling, natural-language tokens, and protein/DNA/RNA sequence design — discovered, scored, ranked, and evidence-linked.
